$statusLegend.ported: byte-equivalent to upstream (CI fails on drift)
$statusLegend.adapted: TypeScript port and/or namespace swap; logically equivalent
$statusLegend.extended: fork adds behavior on top of upstream
$statusLegend.added: file unique to the fork (no upstream)

files.notes: name + textdomain swapped to the artisanpack namespace. comment-template PROVIDES the per-comment block contexts (artisanpack/commentId, artisanpack/commentPreview) that the leaf comment-* display blocks consume — set via the providesContext map in block.json. usesContext keeps the upstream postId / postType pair. Attribute schema and supports carried verbatim for parity.

Loop wrapper. Renders <InnerBlocks /> with a default per-iteration template (comment-author-name + comment-date + comment-content + comment-reply-link). createCommentPlaceholderEdit is intentionally not used here — the wrapper doesn't preview comment data; its leaf children do. The server-side CommentInliner clones the saved template once per resolved comment and CommentResolver stamps each iteration.
Dynamic block (or InnerBlocks delegate for wrappers) — markup produced server-side from stamped _resolved* attributes.
Bidirectional core/comment-template ↔ artisanpack/comment-template block transforms for the rollout window.
Inline SVG inserter icon (editor lacks dashicons.css).
Adapted to the visual-editor auto-discovery contract.

knownDivergences: edit.tsx renders <InnerBlocks /> with a default per-iteration template rather than upstream's entity-querying loop edit — the server-side CommentInliner expands the saved template once per resolved comment instead.
Server-rendered: front-end markup produced by Blade/React/Vue renderers from stamped _resolved* attributes, not by save().
transforms.ts adds bidirectional block transforms that do not exist upstream — required to coexist with core/comment-template during the V1 rollout.
